inputs.reasoning: Alternation with repetition exercises the kInstAlt opcode and switch(arg) cases in the BitState engine, hitting the alt-matching logic.
Named captures with kInstCapture opcode and arg==0 case, testing capture register logic.
Nested repetition creates complex NFA requiring multiple job stack pushes and the while(njob_ > 0) loop in TrySearch.
Large counted repetition with range forces many Push operations, potentially triggering GrowStack when njob_ >= maxjob_.
Character classes with repetition exercise kInstByteRange opcode and the p < end boundary check.
Multiple alternation branches test kInstAltMatch and greedy vs non-greedy matching logic.
Sequence of repetitions creates many sequential instructions, exercising the main TrySearch loop and CheckAndLoop label.
Deeply nested repetitions stress the job stack with many Push calls, testing GrowStack and job array management.
Combined alternation and repetition with different quantifiers exercises multiple alt-matching code paths and arg switching.
Negated character class with perl classes exercises ByteRange matching and boundary conditions in the state machine.
